content: I've been thinking a lot about the wildfires we've had in Yellowstone this season. It's been really tough on the wildlife and the park infrastructure.
That sounds incredibly challenging. As a park ranger, you must be on the front lines dealing with these issues. How has the situation been affecting your work?

It's been intense. We've had to evacuate visitors multiple times and our firefighting team has been working around the clock. I'm actually a volunteer firefighter too, so I've been pulling double duty.
That's remarkable dedication. I can't imagine the stress of managing both roles during such a crisis. Have you seen any particular impact on the ecosystem that concerns you most?

The wildlife displacement is heartbreaking. I saw a mother bear and her cubs wandering near the visitor center last week, which is completely out of character. And the air quality has been terrible - I've had to keep my rabbit Coco inside for weeks.
That must be so difficult to witness. I didn't know you had a rabbit - what's Coco like? And how are you managing to protect your own health with all the smoke?

Coco's a Netherland Dwarf, super energetic. I've got HEPA filters running 24/7 in my house. Honestly, I'm thinking about moving to Melbourne, Australia. My sister lives there and says the air quality is much better.
That's a big decision. What's drawing you to Melbourne specifically? And how would such a move affect your work as a park ranger?

My sister's been there for 5 years now and loves it. Plus, I've always wanted to experience living abroad. I'd probably look for similar work with Parks Victoria. I've been a ranger for 12 years now, so I have the experience.
Twelve years is quite an accomplishment. What do you enjoy most about being a park ranger? And have you started the process of looking into opportunities in Australia?

I love the connection to nature and educating visitors about conservation. I haven't started the process yet - been too busy with the fires. But I'm planning to reach out to Parks Victoria next month when things hopefully calm down.
